key_insight: The paper improves nonlinear elimination preconditioning by learning the slowly converging “bad” residual components from dominant residual structure rather than selecting them directly from noisy raw residuals.
engineering_relevance: This makes Newton solvers for large nonlinear PDE systems more robust and less sensitive to manual thresholds, which is valuable for scalable CFD and other multiphysics simulations.
method: Collect residual snapshots during Newton stagnation phases.
Train an unsupervised extractor (PCA or autoencoder) to reconstruct dominant residual structure.
Use the reconstructed residuals to identify the bad subset inside a parallel domain decomposition framework.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports lid-driven cavity flow tests up to Reynolds number 10,000, where the learned method produced more coherent bad subsets, was robust to parameter choices, and outperformed the baseline NE preconditioner.
watch_item: The approach adds online training and subdomain model fitting, so performance may depend on snapshot quality, stagnation detection, and the extra computational cost of learning.
paper_title: A domain decomposition online-learning-enhanced nonlinear elimination preconditioner
